Iran has transmitted its response to the latest U.S. proposal to end the war to Pakistani mediators, Iran’s state news agency IRNA reported Sunday.

According to IRNA, the proposal under discussion focuses at this stage exclusively on ending the war in the region — with broader issues to be addressed in subsequent negotiations.

Iran’s Foreign Ministry spokesperson had previously said Tehran’s response would be sent once its review and final assessment were complete.

Diesel's Share in Indian PV Market Holds Steady at ~18% for 3 Consecutive Years; SUVs & Luxury Vehicles Keep the Fuel Alive Despite Urban Shift to Petrol/CNG/EV

Market Share — Diesel in Indian PV Market
Diesel PV share: 17.8% (FY24) → 18.2% (FY25) → 18.4% (FY26) — remarkably stable

Peak was 47% in 2012-13 — fallen sharply due to tighter emission norms, petrol/CNG/EV push

Diesel cars sold: 7,58,000 (FY24) → 7,96,000 (FY25) → 8,74,000 (FY26) — volumes actually growing

Who's Still Buying Diesel
SUV enthusiasts, off-roaders, and long-distance cruisers remain loyal

Diesel no longer relevant for entry-level urban buyers

Concentrated in larger vehicles where pulling power, highway efficiency & range trump fuel-type preference

Fuel Share in FY26 Sales — OEM-wise
Luxury OEMs:
Mercedes 55.9% mix
BMW 26.5%
Land Rover 17.4%

Mass-market OEMs:
Mahindra 57.3% — highest among mass-market
Hyundai 14.1%
Kia 10.5%

Key Risk — BS7 on the Horizon
BS7 emission standards set to sharply raise costs

Next regulatory cycle could determine whether diesel's SUV stronghold survives or finally breaks

Small diesel vehicles already economically unviable for most automakers

Winner: Mahindra & Mahindra
Scorpio, Thar, Bolero seeing strong demand on diesel powertrains

M&M positioned as the prime beneficiary of diesel's SUV concentration trend

Core Theme 
Diesel in India has completed a structural retreat — from mass-market staple to a concentrated SUV & luxury niche — but within that niche it is holding firm and even growing in volume; the real inflection point will be BS7, which could either permanently entrench diesel as a premium-SUV-only fuel or finally break its last stronghold.

ATLANTA ELECTRICALS FY26 MANAGEMENT HIGHLIGHTS

FY26 Financial Performance
- FY26 Revenue ₹1,851.5 Cr (↑ 48.8% YoY)  
- EBITDA ₹344.4 Cr  
- EBITDA Margin 18.6%  
- PAT ₹201.8 Cr (↑ 70.1% YoY)  

- Q4FY26 marked the highest-ever quarterly performance for the company  

Business & Operational Highlights
- FY26 described as a landmark year following listing on BSE & NSE  
- Manufacturing capacity expanded to 63,060 MVA during FY26  

- Company progressing toward higher voltage transformer capabilities:
  - 400 kV transformers  
  - 765 kV transformers  

- First launches expected during FY27  

Expansion & Growth Initiatives
- Operations to commence at:
  - Inverter Duty Transformer facility  
  - Backward integration plant  

- Management targeting opportunities across:
  - Transmission infrastructure  
  - Renewable energy  
  - BESS (Battery Energy Storage Systems)  
  - Data centers  
  - Export markets  

Industry Opportunity
- Company aiming to benefit from India’s ~₹9 trillion transmission investment pipeline  
- Growing grid modernization and renewable integration expected to support transformer demand over long term  

Balance Sheet Highlights
- Company fully deleveraged during FY26  
- Complete repayment of:
  - Vadod acquisition term loans  
  - BTW acquisition term loans  

- Stronger balance sheet expected to improve:
  - Financial flexibility  
  - Expansion capability  
  - Return ratios  

Execution Milestone
- Received PGCIL approval for manufacturing transformers up to 400 kV class at Vadod facility  
- Approval achieved within two years of groundbreaking, reflecting strong execution capability  

Outlook
- Management optimistic on:
  - Capacity utilization improvement  
  - Export growth  
  - Higher voltage transformer opportunity  
  - Renewable and transmission sector demand tailwinds  

Final Takeaway
- Atlanta Electricals is entering a new growth phase supported by strong financial performance, debt-free balance sheet, capacity expansion, higher voltage product entry and long-term opportunities in transmission, renewables and grid infrastructure  

Impact
- Positive

• 🏛️ Securities Appellate Tribunal (SAT), Mumbai dismissed Jetking Infotrain’s appeal against BSE order dated May 8, 2026

• 📌 SAT upheld BSE’s rejection of the listing application for preferential allotment shares

• 📝 Matter relates to alleged non-compliance in amendment of Memorandum of Association (MoA) linked to the preferential issue

• ⚠️ SAT observed company used preferential issue proceeds to invest in VDAs before RoC certification of amended MoA

• 📅 Company allegedly filed listing application before amendment approval process was fully completed

• 💰 No monetary penalty or fine imposed by SAT; order carries no cost

• ⚖️ Company evaluating further legal remedies against the SAT order

🔴 Impact: Negative (Preferential shares listing setback and governance/compliance concerns may weigh on sentiment)

Sambhv Steel Tubes - Q4 FY26 Result Analysis

Q3 was a margin trough. Q4 was a full reversal. And the Kesda greenfield changes the scale of this company entirely.

1) The Q4 recovery story

EBITDA/ton in Q3: ₹5,200. EBITDA/ton in Q4: ₹8,559. Management had guided ₹7,500. They beat it by 14%. EBITDA margin: 13.46%, 375 bps better than Q4 FY25 and 480 bps better than Q3. Revenue grew 38.4% YoY to ₹685 Cr. Record quarterly volumes of 1.08 lakh tons, above the guided 1–1.5 lakh ton range. VAP mix at 89% of total volume. Q4 was the clean quarter the business needed after a difficult Q3.

2) What drove the recovery

Management had said on the Q3 call that falling iron ore and sponge iron costs would show in Q4. They did. Material costs as a share of revenue rose slightly to 74.8% from 70.6% YoY, some raw material pressure remained but operating leverage from higher volumes and cost control on employee and other expenses offset it. Finance costs fell 41.1% YoY as IPO-funded debt repayment took effect.

3) The IPO-funded balance sheet transformation

Total debt cut 26% YoY from ₹503 Cr to ₹371 Cr. D/E fell from 1.02x to 0.35x. Finance cost down 41% YoY in Q4. ₹390 Cr prepaid from IPO proceeds. CFO grew 68% YoY to ₹212 Cr. CFO/PAT of 1.49x, strong cash quality. Net worth more than doubled to ₹1,054 Cr. The company enters FY27 with a clean balance sheet to fund aggressive capex.

4) The governance flag - small but worth noting

A ₹3.5 Cr provision for a fraudulent land purchase at the subsidiary. ₹11.5 Cr was advanced, ₹8 Cr recovered, ₹3.5 Cr provided for. FIR lodged. Financially immaterial but it's a fraud event and a reminder that governance at the subsidiary level needs watching as the company scales.

5) The FY28 thesis: Kesda is everything

FY26 revenue: ₹2,413 Cr. FY28 target: ₹4,500 Cr. Implied CAGR: ~37% over two years. That's not achievable from organic brownfield expansion alone. The Kesda greenfield, 350k TPA Phase 1, commissioning expected Q4 FY27, revenue potential of ₹2,400–2,800 Cr is the thesis in one asset. If Kesda commissions on time and ramps, FY28 is achievable. If it slips, it isn't.

New capex of ₹200 Cr also announced - 150k TPA pipe mill and 30MW captive power plant at Sarora, Raipur, targeted by December 2027.

6) Guidance scorecard:

Q4 volume 1–1.5 L tons → 1.08 L tons ✅
Q4 EBITDA/ton ₹7,500 → ₹8,559 ✅
FY26 EBITDA/ton ₹7,000 → ₹6,962 ✅
Q4 EBITDA margin 13% → 13.46% ✅

7) What to watch in FY27:

Kesda commissioning timeline is the single most important variable. Every quarter of delay pushes the FY28 target further out of reach. Watch the CWIP drawdown and any management commentary on commissioning dates.

The Development
Tech giant Google exploring investment opportunities in India across:
AI infrastructure — data centres, AI servers, cloud infrastructure
Manufacturing of servers and drones

Union IT & Electronics Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw disclosed this after meeting Google executives:
Wilson L White — VP, Global Affairs
Kara Bailey — VP, Android and Play Strategy

No investment size, timeline, possible manufacturing locations, or whether discussions have progressed beyond exploratory stage disclosed

Strategic Context — India's AI Infrastructure Push
Centre stepping up efforts to build domestic AI computing capacity and attract investments into higher-value electronics manufacturing segments beyond smartphones

India increasingly framing AI infrastructure as a strategic national capability amid rising global demand for computing power to train and deploy generative AI models

Push to position India as a base for AI-linked infrastructure and advanced electronics manufacturing

What AI Investments Typically Involve
Industry executives: AI investments typically involve large-scale data centres, specialised AI servers, networking equipment, and cloud infrastructure

Broader Momentum
Announcement follows groundbreaking of a proposed $15 billion AI hub project in Visakhapatnam earlier this year

India pushing beyond smartphones into AI and advanced electronics manufacturing as the next phase of the electronics PLI story

Core Theme
Google's exploration of AI infrastructure and drone/server manufacturing investments in India — coming on the heels of the $15 billion Visakhapatnam AI hub — signals that India is successfully repositioning itself from a smartphone assembly destination to a serious contender for global AI infrastructure capex, with the government actively using high-level ministerial engagement to convert exploratory discussions into committed investments.

reports.text:
India's Going-Out Economy Turns into a Platform Battle; District Scales to 12 Mn Weekly Users, Challenging BookMyShow's Monopoly

The Disruption
India's online ticketing — long dominated by BookMyShow — is now a multi-player battleground

District (backed by Zomato): scaled from 3 million to 12 million weekly active users in under a year

District reported revenue of more than ₹970 crore in its first full financial year

Has led BookMyShow in monthly app downloads since May 2025

Flipkart (Walmart-owned) reportedly preparing to enter the segment — raising the prospect of ecommerce, food delivery, and entertainment platforms colliding in India's hottest discretionary spending category

BookMyShow — Still Large but Pressured
Had ~12 million weekly active users (May 2025, Sensor Tower) vs District's 3 million then — gap has now closed entirely

Live events business revenue: ₹756 crore in FY25 — nearing its ₹828 crore ticketing business

For years operated from a position of overwhelming strength — shaping convenience fees, exclusivity clauses, and revenue-sharing arrangements

That balance of power beginning to shift

The Platform Bundling War
Both District and BookMyShow moving from software toward physical event assets

Platforms increasingly bundling: movies, dining, sports, and retail into single consumer ecosystems

District: integrated ticketing with Zomato's restaurant network, sports bookings through Play, offline retail through Stores

District Pass membership bundles all offerings — creating a larger lifestyle ecosystem

Platforms now seen as gateways into broader urban spending habits, not just ticketing tools

Data as the Moat
EY India partner Ashish Pherwani: "These platforms have huge amounts of data. They know exactly who watched the first part of a franchise and how to reach them for the second"

Eating-out and food delivery data could be utilised for selling event tickets — Zomato's District has a structural advantage here

IPL as Battleground
District: exclusive ticketing partner for 5 of IPL's 10 franchises — handles ticketing for ~35 league matches

BookMyShow: handles ~25 matches
"Two seasons ago, such competition barely existed"

Infrastructure Gap — India's Structural Challenge
Unlike London or Dubai, India lacks permanent large-scale concert infrastructure

Organisers forced to repeatedly construct temporary venues and staging systems

Next phase of the battle may depend less on app downloads and more on who builds the physical backbone of India's live-events industry

Core Theme
India's going-out economy is no longer BookMyShow's to define — District's explosive growth from 3 to 12 million weekly users, Flipkart's imminent entry, and the convergence of food delivery, ecommerce, and entertainment into lifestyle super-apps is turning online ticketing into India's next major platform war, where data, physical asset ownership, and ecosystem bundling will matter far more than app downloads alone.

reports.text:
India's GLP-1 Market Triples to ₹1,736 Crore in April 2026; Generic Semaglutide Surge Drives Volume Even as Innovator Revenues Slip

Market Size — Explosive Growth
India GLP-1 market: ₹1,736 crore in April 2026 — more than tripled from ₹545 crore a year earlier

Market size trajectory: ₹444 crore (2024) → ₹545 crore (2025) → ₹1,736 crore (April 2026)

Growth driven by: cheaper generic semaglutide launches, wider concept acceptance, user friendliness of injectable form

Volume Surge — Semaglutide Generics Leading
Combined sales of four innovator semaglutide brands (Wegovy, Ozempic, Poviztra, Extensior): delivered ~40% volume growth in April over March

Wegovy sales: 17,000 units in April — up from 10,000 units a month ago

Semaglutide generics volume: surged 238% in April over March — reaching 1,37,000 units

Zydus Lifesciences and Lupin gaining significant generic share

Innovator Revenue — Declining Despite Volume Growth
Semaglutide (Innovators) monthly sales: ₹135 crore (Feb) → ₹114 crore (Mar) → ₹121 crore (Apr)

Revenue declining because Novo Nordisk reduced price of:
Ozempic starting dose: by 36%
Wegovy starting dose: by 48%

Despite price cuts, innovator brands retaining position — "stickiness of doctors and patients to originators"

Generics — Market Share Battle
Torrent Pharmaceuticals: emerged as market leader in generics — controlling ~38% of overall semaglutide generics market and 28% share in injectable semaglutide generics

Torrent currently only company offering oral generic semaglutide in India — through Semalix and Sembolic

Generic semaglutide sales in April surged through Semalix and Sembolic

Tirzepatide (Eli Lilly) — Under Pressure
Shortly after generic semaglutide launch, tirzepatide took a hit

Monthly sales of Eli Lilly's Mounjaro dropped 21% in March

Tirzepatide innovator volume: 92,000 (Feb) → 77,000 (Mar) → 83,000 (Apr)

Eli Lilly's Mounjaro and Yurpeak (Lilly-Cipla collaboration): recorded modest 6% growth in value in April vs March

Key Market Insights
VP (Commercial) Pharmarack Sheetal Sapale: "Broadly, the innovators in both semaglutide and tirzepatide segments have managed to retain their position well despite the generic onslaught. 

Steady growth of innovators also indicates stickiness of a class of doctors and patients to originators"

Eli Lilly's tirzepatide injections (launched 2025) propelled GLP-1 segment growth — generics then expanded the market itself

Novo Nordisk sells Wegovy, Ozempic, and Rybelsus directly in India; partnered with Emcure for Poviztra and Abbott for Extensior

Core Theme
India's GLP-1 market is undergoing a structural demand explosion — tripling to ₹1,736 crore in April 2026 — driven by generic semaglutide democratising access at lower price points, even as innovator brands retain physician loyalty and volume growth, setting up a classic pharmaceutical market expansion playbook where generics grow the pie while originators protect their premium positioning.

Hyundai India Q4 FY26 PAT Down 22.2% YoY to ₹1,256 Crore; Misses Street on Commodity Costs and Product Mix; Plans ₹7,500 Crore Capex in FY27

Q4 FY26 Financials — Miss on All Metrics
Net Profit: ₹1,256 crore, down 22.2% YoY (vs ₹1,614 crore) — missed Bloomberg estimate of ₹1,268 crore

Revenue: ₹18,916 crore, up 5.4% YoY (vs ₹17,940 crore) — missed estimate of ₹19,197 crore

EBITDA: ₹1,966 crore, down 22.4% YoY (vs ₹2,533 crore)

EBITDA margin: 10.4% — narrowed sharply from 14.1% in Q4 FY25

Net profit margin: 6.6% vs 9.0% a year ago

What Went Wrong
Rising commodity costs — key drag on margins

Weaker product mix — shift in sales mix towards lower-margin sedans and hatchbacks instead of higher-margin SUVs

Intensifying domestic competition — Hyundai slipped to 4th position in PV market in FY26 (from 2nd spot earlier)

Before GST reform, mix was heavily skewed toward cars over 4 meters — now seeing growth across all segments

FY26 Full Year Volume Data
Total sales: 7,75,031 units, up 1.7% YoY

Export volumes: grew 16.4% to 1,90,125 units

Domestic sales: declined 2.3% to 5,84,906 units

SUVs: 68% of domestic sales — led by Creta, Venue, and Exter

Rural market penetration: record 25% in March quarter

CNG contribution: rose to 18%

Alternative fuel vehicles showing strong traction

FY27 Outlook & Strategy
Revenue guidance: 8–10% growth in both domestic sales and exports

Profitability expected to improve on back of new product launches, operating leverage, and stronger export mix

Two new nameplates planned: one ICE SUV and one electric SUV

MD & CEO Tarun Garg: "We have every intention to come back to the number two position… we are very passionate about our position and we will get it back sooner than later"

Capex — Highest Ever
FY27 capex: ₹7,500 crore — highest-ever annual investment in India

Aimed at: expanding manufacturing capacity and supporting SUV and EV pipeline

Plans to increase total installed manufacturing capacity to 1.14 million units through phased Pune facility expansion

Current capacity: 9,94,000 units (Chennai: 8,24,000 + Pune: 1,70,000)

Pune plant to scale to 2,50,000 units initially → 3,20,000 units in second phase

CAFE 3 Compliance
Company confident of meeting upcoming Corporate Average Fuel Efficiency (CAFE 3) norms as it strengthens product portfolio and compliance strategy

Core Theme
Hyundai India's Q4 FY26 miss — with margins collapsing from 14.1% to 10.4% on commodity headwinds and mix deterioration — marks the low point of a difficult year that saw the company slip to 4th in the domestic market, but management's aggressive ₹7,500 crore capex plan, two new SUV/EV launches, and explicit commitment to reclaim the No. 2 position signal that the recovery strategy is already in motion.

Karur Vysya Bank MD & CEO B Ramesh Babu: Will Raise Corporate Loan Mix to 20% Over 2–3 Years; FY26 PAT at Highest-Ever ₹2,510 Crore

FY26 Record Performance
Net Profit: ₹2,510 crore — highest-ever for the bank

Key drivers:
Progressive shift toward RAM (Retail, Agri, MSME) segment from corporate

Improved CASA mobilisation — CASA deposits grew 12% YoY to ₹31,122 crore

Lower reliance on bulk deposits and certificates of deposits

Changed a few floating-rate loans to fixed-rate — supporting yield on advances

All factors combined to deliver best-ever interest income

NIM Guidance — FY27
FY26 NIM: 3.97%

Adjusted NIM (excluding one-off income tax refund): 4.25% in Q4

FY27 NIM guidance: 3.7–3.8%

Pressure factors: deposits still facing tightness; term deposit interest rates increased in April — cost of deposits likely to go up

Earlier time deposits repriced at lower rate in Q3 — that benefit may not repeat in Q1 FY27

Yields on advances also expected to contract — double pressure on NIM

Corporate Lending — Turning the Corner
Corporate loan share: was 35–40% historically → brought down to 14% currently

Saw continuous degrowth in corporate for last 2–3 years — now reversed

Saw 12% growth in corporate loans in FY26 — first meaningful pickup

Also started investing in credit substitutes

Target: take corporate mix to 20% over next 2–3 years

End-state target loan mix: RAM 80% : Corporate 20%

Liquidity Management
Maintaining LCR at 115–120%

Planning deposit growth in line with LCR requirements

War Impact on MSME Portfolio
No direct impact currently

If an importer is not paying, exporter still has to pay — but collateral protection in place

Second-order risks being watched: logistics, automobile sector, chemical factories, fertilisers, and ceramics could face impact from crude oil price increase

"If at all this precipitates and these people cannot insulate themselves, there can be issues" — watching closely but no stress yet

Core Theme
Karur Vysya Bank is at a strategic pivot point — having successfully transformed from a corporate-heavy lender to a RAM-dominated franchise delivering record profits, it is now carefully re-introducing corporate lending toward a 20% mix target, while managing NIM compression from deposit repricing and navigating second-order geopolitical risks with collateral-backed conservatism.Sonnet 4.6

BenQ India MD Rajeev Singh: Targeting 25% Annual Growth for Next 5 Years; Projectors & India-Designed Products to Drive Next Phase

Growth Strategy
Targeting 25% annual growth sustained over the next 5 years

Growth driven by flat-panel displays, computer monitors, and projectors (commercial + personal)

Deliberate focus on mid- and high-range segments — avoiding entry-level due to low value addition

Consumer behaviour shift from first-time purchases to repeat buying on value, supporting premium positioning

Projector Segment — Key Bet
Projectors called "most underrated product category" — expected to evolve significantly, especially in home segment

Unlike TVs (fixed position), projectors are portable — strong appeal to younger demographic

India projector market could double annually over next few years

Projector segment just beginning to take off in India and globally

Make in India → Designed for India
Strategy evolving from 'Make in India' to 'Designed for India'

Two contract manufacturers in Gujarat and Tirupati (Andhra Pradesh); may invest in own facility if needed

Coding monitors fully researched in India (Hyderabad + Bengaluru) — engaging programmers to understand local needs

Goal: develop India-first products not in international portfolio — "if it succeeds in India, it can do well globally"

Core Theme
BenQ India is executing a deliberate premiumisation play — anchoring in mid-to-high displays while betting heavily on projectors as the next mass category, and shifting from contract manufacturing to India-originated R&D, positioning India as both a high-growth market and a global product innovation hub.

Titan Q4FY26: Net Profit Jumps 35% to ₹1,179 Crore; Revenue Surges 46% to ₹20,300 Crore as Jewellery & Watches Fire on All Cylinders

Headline Numbers
Net Profit: ₹1,179 crore, up 35% YoY

Total Income: ₹20,300 crore, up 46% YoY

FY26 full-year total income: ₹76,078 crore, up 33% YoY

FY26 full-year PAT: ₹5,073 crore, up 52% YoY

Segment-Wise Revenue
Jewellery: ₹18,195 crore — up 50% YoY (excl. bullion & digi-gold)

Watches (Analog): ₹18,195 crore — up 15% YoY, driven by premiumisation

Eyewear: ₹227 crore — up 17% YoY

Jewellery — Deep Dive
India jewellery business (Tanishq + Mia + Zoya): ₹17,114 crore, up 46% YoY

Combined domestic portfolio grew 48% to ₹16,047 crore

CaratLane: ₹1,066 crore, up 22% YoY

International jewellery: ₹1,081 crore, up 174% YoY — double-digit retail growth in GCC and North America

Strategic Move — Damas Acquisition
Completed 67% stake in Damas Jewellery (GCC market)

Added 123 stores to Titan's network — significant overseas expansion milestone

Also opened 4 new stores in GCC during the quarter

Retail Expansion
27 new stores added in India — 8 Tanishq, 14 Mia, 5 CaratLane

Tanishq also expanded in GCC region

Weak Spot
Smart watches: nearly 50% decline in overall value — category remains under pressure

Milestone
Crossed ₹50,000 crore annual revenue in FY25 after 40 years — then added next ₹25,000 crore in a single year (FY26)

Core Theme
Titan delivered a landmark FY26 — jewellery momentum at elevated gold prices, analog watch premiumisation, and the transformative Damas acquisition collectively re-rated the growth curve; the smartwatch drag is the only blemish in an otherwise broad-based quarter that sets up aggressive international expansion as the next compounding lever.

Global crude and fuel prices surged following the Iran-West Asia conflict

• 💰 Average total deposits increase 13% YoY to ₹2,801 Cr

reports.text:
ISGEC HEAVY ENGINEERING TO SELL 25% STAKE IN JV FOR ₹4 CR 🤝🏭

• 🏢 ISGEC Heavy Engineering to sell 25% stake in JV arm Isgec SFW Boilers Pvt Ltd to joint venture partner Sumitomo SHI FW Energia OY

• 💰 Transaction value stands at ₹4 Cr

• 📉 Post transaction, ISGEC’s stake in JV will reduce from 51% to 26%

• 🔄 JV company will cease to be a subsidiary and become an associate company after completion

• 📅 Deal expected to be completed by June 30, 2026

• 🤝 JV partnership between ISGEC and Sumitomo SHI FW Energia OY will continue post transaction

• 📊 JV contributed only ~0.18% of consolidated turnover and ~0.29% of net worth in FY25

🟡 Impact: Neutral to Slightly Positive (Strategic stake rationalisation with limited financial impact on consolidated business)

DALMIA BHARAT DENIES MEDIA REPORTS ON SFIO MATTER ⚖️📰

• 🏢 Dalmia Bharat issues clarification on media/social media reports related to alleged fraudulent transfer of mutual fund units by Allied Financial Services

• 📌 Company says reports are speculative, motivated & denied contents of such articles

• 🏦 Subsidiary Dalmia Cement Bharat had earlier alleged misappropriation of securities from its demat account by Allied

• ⚖️ Company highlighted that EOW, SEBI & SFIO had investigated the matter and taken actions against Allied & related entities

• 🚫 Dalmia Bharat states neither DCBL nor its associate companies have received any SFIO report

• 🏛️ Company also noted Supreme Court had released mutual funds to DCBL in 2019

• 📢 Management reiterated commitment to corporate governance, transparency & legal compliance

🟡 Impact: Neutral (Primarily a clarification statement; company attempting to address market concerns and deny speculative reports)

reports.text:

• 🏛️ NCLAT directs Interim Resolution Professional (IRP) not to take further steps in CIRP proceedings against Prime Focus

• 📌 Relief granted pending next hearing in appeal filed by the company

• ⚖️ Matter relates to earlier NCLT Mumbai order admitting insolvency petition under Section 7 of IBC

• 📅 Appeal was heard by NCLAT on May 8, 2026

• ⏳ Next hearing in the matter scheduled for May 11, 2026

• 🛑 Interim stay on further CIRP actions provides temporary operational relief to the company

🟢 Impact: Positive (Interim relief reduces immediate insolvency-related uncertainty & offers operational breathing space)

• 🔄 Hotel is upgraded & rebranded version of Four Points by Sheraton Kochi Infopark

• 🛏️ Property features 218 rooms & suites targeting corporate, MICE & leisure travellers

• 📍 Located in Kakkanad, adjoining Infopark Kochi in a key IT corridor

• ✈️ Hotel positioned near SmartCity, business hubs & Kochi International Airport

• 🍽️ Property includes multiple dining options, rooftop infinity pool, spa, fitness centre & large meeting/event spaces

• 🌍 Hotel integrated with Marriott Bonvoy loyalty ecosystem for global customer reach

• 📈 Management says move aligns with strategy of upgrading hospitality assets in strong business locations

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens premium hospitality portfolio, enhances brand positioning & improves long-term revenue potential)

• 📦 Desco Infratech receives purchase order from KP Energy worth ₹1.92 Cr (inclusive of GST)

• 🔧 Order involves supply & installation of underground cable-related materials and execution works

• 🇮🇳 Contract awarded by a domestic entity

• 🏭 Company also announced successful completion of commissioning & gasification activities at Vizag Steel Plant under IOCL-associated Vizag GA project

• 📈 Company believes project execution strengthens its presence in City Gas Distribution (CGD) & industrial gas infrastructure segment

🟢 Impact: Positive (Order inflow and successful project commissioning support execution track record & business visibility)

PHYSICSWALLAH PARTNERS WITH VI FOR DIGITAL LEARNING PLANS 📚📱

• 🤝 PhysicsWallah partners with Vi (Vodafone Idea) to launch bundled EdTech + connectivity offering under Vi Edu+ prepaid plans

• 📍 Initial rollout in Rajasthan & UP East circles, with plans to expand to other regions later

• 📖 PhysicsWallah’s “Pi” educational OTT platform to be bundled with select Vi prepaid recharge plans

• 🎯 Students will get access to content for JEE, NEET, UPSC, Board exams & other competitive exams

• 📶 Plans include unlimited 4G/5G data, voice calls, SMS benefits & 28-day access to PW Pi Pro

• 🎓 PW says platform includes 30,000+ video lectures, AI-powered doubt solving, quizzes & performance tracking tools

• 💡 Companies aim to improve affordable digital education access, especially in emerging & underserved markets

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strategic telecom-EdTech collaboration expands user reach, boosts digital engagement & strengthens brand ecosystem)

reports.text:
PNC INFRATECH RECEIVES PCOD FOR ₹819 CR HAM ROAD PROJECT 🛣️🏗️

• 🏛️ PNC Infratech receives Provisional Completion Certificate (PCOD) for HAM project in Uttar Pradesh

• 🛣️ Project executed by subsidiary Prayagraj Kaushambi Highway Package 3 Pvt Ltd

• 📍 Project involves 4-laning of NH-731A from Ganga Bridge to Banarpur Kadipur Ichauli in Uttar Pradesh

• 💰 Project bid cost stands at ₹819 Cr plus price index multiple as per concession agreement

• 📅 Project achieved provisional completion on March 31, 2026

• 🚦 Commercial operations eligible to commence with effect from March 31, 2026

• ⏳ Project completed within 90-day grace period from scheduled completion date

• 🤝 Authority is Ministry of Road Transport & Highways (MoRTH) through UPPWD under HAM mode

🟢 Impact: Positive (Completion milestone enables revenue recognition/annuity inflows & strengthens execution track record)

reports.text:
KPI GREEN BAGS ₹621 CR NTPC RENEWABLE SOLAR PROJECT ☀️⚡

• 🏗️ KPI Green Energy receives Notifications of Award (NOAs) from NTPC Renewable Energy Ltd for 500 MW Solar BOS project in Rajasthan

• 📍 Project pertains to development of grid-connected solar PV projects at Bikaner, Rajasthan

• 💰 Aggregate contract value stands at ₹621 Cr excluding GST

• ☀️ Order divided into two blocks — ₹367 Cr for 300 MW block & ₹254 Cr for 200 MW block

• 🔧 Scope includes supply of plant & equipment, installation, testing, commissioning, civil & allied works

• 🛠️ Contract also includes comprehensive O&M services for 3 years plus 10-year AMC

• 🌍 Company says award strengthens presence in renewable-rich states & expands geographical footprint

• 🇮🇳 Aggregate project size under the award is 500 MW

🟢 Impact: Strong Positive (Large NTPC renewable order boosts revenue visibility, strengthens solar EPC portfolio & supports long-term growth)

• 🇮🇳 Project awarded by a domestic entity

• 🤝 No promoter group or related party interest involved

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens railway infrastructure order pipeline & improves execution visibility)

reports.text:
HYUNDAI MOTOR INDIA FY27 OUTLOOK

Growth Guidance
- Domestic volume growth expected at 8%–10% in FY27  
- Export volume growth also guided at 8%–10%  

Capex Plans
- Planned capital expenditure of around ₹7,500 Cr  
- Investments aimed at:
  - Capacity enhancement  
  - Product development  
  - EV ecosystem expansion  

Margin Guidance
- EBITDA margin expected in the range of 11%–14% for FY27  

Product Launch Pipeline
- Two completely new vehicle nameplates planned in FY27:
  - One focused on strengthening Hyundai’s mid-SUV portfolio  
  - One localized dedicated EV in the compact SUV category  

Strategic Focus
- Expanding SUV portfolio across segments  
- Increasing localization and EV penetration  
- Strengthening domestic and export market positioning  

EV Strategy
- Upcoming compact SUV EV marks Hyundai’s push toward localized electric mobility platform development in India  

Final Takeaway
- Hyundai is entering FY27 with a strong product pipeline, aggressive capex plans and balanced focus on domestic growth, exports and EV expansion  

Impact
- Positive

UJJIVAN SMALL FINANCE BANK Q4FY26

Business Growth
- Gross Loan Book ₹40,655 Cr (↑ 26.6% YoY & ↑ 9.7% QoQ)  
  - Group Loans ₹14,696 Cr (↑ 12% YoY & ↑ 7% QoQ)  

- Secured mix improved to 49.4% vs 43.5% YoY & 48.1% QoQ  
- Secured loan book growth (↑ 44% YoY)  
- Unsecured loan book growth (↑ 13.5% YoY)  

Disbursements
- Highest-ever quarterly disbursements ₹9,811 Cr (↑ 32.1% YoY & ↑ 18.3% QoQ)  
- Group Loan disbursements ₹3,870 Cr (↑ 39% YoY & ↑ 12% QoQ)  

Deposits
- Total Deposits ₹45,668 Cr (↑ 21.4% YoY & ↑ 8.2% QoQ)  
- CASA Deposits ₹13,062 Cr (↑ 35.9% YoY)  
- CASA Ratio improved to 28.6%  

Profitability
- NII ₹1,092 Cr (↑ 26.4% YoY & ↑ 9.2% QoQ)  
- Total Income ₹2,186 Cr (↑ 19% YoY & ↑ 7% QoQ)  
- PPOP ₹515 Cr (↑ 43% YoY & ↑ 17% QoQ)  

- Credit Cost ₹144 Cr (↓ 46% YoY & ↓ 26% QoQ)  
- PAT ₹282 Cr (↑ 238.2% YoY & ↑ 51.6% QoQ)  

Margins & Returns
- NIM 8.5% vs 8.3% YoY & 8.2% QoQ  
- ROA 2.1% vs 0.7% YoY & 1.5% QoQ  
- ROE 17.2% vs 5.6% YoY & 11.5% QoQ  

Asset Quality
- GNPA 2.26% vs 2.18% YoY & 2.38% QoQ  
- NNPA 0.43% vs 0.49% YoY & 0.57% QoQ  
- Portfolio at Risk (PAR) 3.54% vs 4.54% YoY & 3.98% QoQ  
- Collection Efficiency 99.8%  
- Provision Coverage Ratio improved to 81%  

Strategic Developments
- RBI returned application for voluntary transition into Universal Bank  
- RBI acknowledged ongoing diversification efforts  
- Bank plans to reapply after further diversification progress  

Diversification Strategy
- Secured loans now nearly half of total loan book  
- Strong growth in:
  - Gold loans  
  - Vehicle loans  
  - Agriculture loans  

- Newer businesses contribution increased to ~6% of loan mix vs ~3% YoY  

Funding & Capital
- Cost of funds reduced to 7.0% in Q4FY26 (↓ 63 bps YoY)  
- Board approved equity raise of up to ₹2,000 Cr to support future growth  

FY27 Guidance
- Loan book growth around 25%  
- Credit Cost guidance: 1.4%–1.5% of average GLB  
- ROA guidance around 1.6%  

Final Takeaway
- Ujjivan delivered strong growth, improving profitability and better asset quality while steadily transitioning toward a more diversified and secured lending franchise aligned with its long-term universal banking ambitions  

Impact
- Positive

- 𝗦𝗵𝗮𝗿𝗲𝗵𝗼𝗹𝗱𝗲𝗿 𝗘𝗻𝘁𝗶𝘁𝗹𝗲𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁: Existing shareholders of Kaushalaya Logistics Ltd will receive shares in the new demerged entity in a 1:1 ratio. This means for every share held in Kaushalaya Logistics, shareholders will receive one share in the new company

LUPIN MANAGEMENT COMMENTARY

US Business & FY27 Outlook
- Management expects US business revenue to cross $1 Bn in FY27  
- Injectable portfolio expected to remain a key growth driver  
- Core US portfolio seeing stable demand with volume growth offsetting pricing pressure  

Margin Guidance
- FY27 EBITDA margin guidance maintained around 25%  
- Management confident of sustaining profitability despite external headwinds  

Key Product Performance
- Strong performance from:
  - Tolvaptan  
  - Mirabegron  
  - Spiriva  
  - Albuterol  

- Base products continued to witness healthy volume growth in the US market  

Margin Headwinds
- Management highlighted possible pressure from:
  - Higher air freight costs  
  - Elevated ocean freight expenses  
  - Logistics-related disruptions in global markets  

Semaglutide Update
- Only 9 days of Semaglutide sales contributed in Q4FY26  
- April sales trend reported as healthy  
- Early rollout witnessing strong traction in India  

International Expansion
- Semaglutide launch in Canada expected in FY28  
- Company evaluating two large acquisitions focused on:
  - India business  
  - Specialty segment expansion  

Strategic Focus
- Continued push toward:
  - Specialty products  
  - Injectables  
  - Biosimilars  
  - Complex generics  

- Pegfilgrastim launch planned in biosimilars segment  

Final Takeaway
- Lupin is entering FY27 with strong momentum in the US business, improving specialty mix and early success in Semaglutide, while maintaining focus on margins and global expansion opportunities  

Impact
- Positive

Demand Outlook
- FY27 electricity demand growth projected at 5% to 5.5%  
- Sharp recovery from around 1% growth in FY26  
- FY26 demand impacted by weather related disruptions and lower thermal utilisation  

Key Demand Drivers
- Higher agricultural and household consumption due to possible sub-par rainfall and El Niño risk  
- Rising industrial activity  
- Growing EV charging infrastructure demand  
- Increasing electricity consumption from data centres  

Thermal Power Revival
- Around 50 GW generation capacity addition expected in FY27  
- Includes nearly 6 GW thermal capacity; balance from renewables  
- Under-construction thermal capacity crossed 45 GW after years of subdued investment  

Thermal PLF Trend
- FY21: 54.5%  
- FY24: 69.1%  
- FY25: 69.5%  
- FY26: 65.7%  
- FY27E: ~65.5%  

- PLFs expected to remain stable despite demand rebound due to fresh thermal additions and renewable penetration  

Investment and Project Activity
- PSU and private sector thermal project announcements increasing  
- State discoms restarting long-term power purchase bidding activity  
- Thermal power regaining importance as reliable base-load generation for grid stability  

Execution Challenges
- Long project gestation periods remain a key risk  
- Boiler, turbine and generator manufacturers operating with elevated order books  

Electricity Pricing Trends
- Average IEX DAM spot tariff declined to ₹3.8/unit in FY26 from ₹4.4/unit in FY25  
- Softer prices due to slower demand growth and higher renewable generation  

Coal and Fuel Position
- Coal stock levels at thermal plants remain comfortable at around 19 days  
- Supported by improved domestic coal availability  

Discom Financial Stress
- Aggregate discom debt declined modestly to ₹7.1 lakh Cr  
- Cash gap still elevated at 30–33 paise per unit  
- Tariff hikes across states remain muted  
- Tariff orders issued in only 17 of 28 states by April 2026  

Key Structural Concerns
- Rising power purchase costs  
- Slow smart metering implementation  
- Weak operational efficiency in distribution segment  
- Limited tariff pass-through mechanisms  

Sector Theme
- India’s power sector is entering a dual-cycle phase:
  - Strong generation demand recovery led by EVs, industry and data centres  
  - Continued financial stress at discom level limiting sector-wide monetisation efficiency  

Final Takeaway
- Power generation and equipment ecosystem likely to benefit from rising demand and thermal revival, while structural discom weakness remains the biggest long-term risk for sector sustainability  

Impact
- Positive for generation, transmission and equipment players  
- Cautious on distribution segment

Future Growth Areas

- Energy trading licences  
- Green hydrogen projects

Business Transformation

WAAREE ENERGY STRATEGIC EXPANSION UPDATE

Long-Term Vision
- Management targeting revenue of ₹1,00,000 Cr within the next 5 years  
- FY26 revenue base around ₹26,500 Cr  
- Transition underway from solar module manufacturer to integrated clean energy infrastructure company  

Business Transformation
- Earlier focus primarily on solar modules  
- Now scaling across:
  - Solar glass  
  - Wafers  
  - Cells  
  - Batteries  
  - Energy Storage Systems (ESS)  
  - Inverters  
  - Hydrogen equipment  

Strategic Goal
- Aim to capture nearly 90% of customer wallet share across the clean energy value chain  
- Building vertically integrated ecosystem to improve margins, scale and customer stickiness  

US Expansion Opportunity
- US market emerging as a major growth driver due to:
  - IRA incentives  
  - Rising AI and data center power demand  
  - Industrial electrification trends  

Capacity Targets
- Targeting 15.4 GW cell manufacturing capacity  
- Targeting 20 GWh battery business by 2028  

Industry Positioning
- Moving beyond solar manufacturing into broader energy infrastructure and storage ecosystem  
- Positioned to benefit from:
  - Renewable energy growth  
  - Grid-scale storage demand  
  - Decarbonization initiatives globally  

Final Takeaway
- Waaree is pursuing an aggressive full-stack clean energy integration strategy with significant optionality across solar, storage and energy infrastructure segments  

Impact
- Positive

Escorts Kubota: Guidance - Concall Update

• Management expects the tractor industry for FY27 to be flattish, with a potential range of 2-3% up or down, anticipating a subdued H2 due to high base effects, subnormal monsoon forecasts, and El Nino impact.

• The company is confident of achieving positive growth and market share improvement in tractor volumes for FY27, irrespective of the industry outlook, driven by recent product launches and channel development.

• The non-tractor agri segment (farm implements) is expected to grow at a 20%+ CAGR over the next three years, supported by product pipeline expansion and channel growth.

• Commodity cost increases are anticipated to be around 5-6% as a percentage of revenue, with a 1.5% price hike already implemented on tractors and construction equipment, which is insufficient to cover the expected increases.

• Normal CapEx for FY27 is projected at INR350-400 crores, with an additional INR500 crores for the greenfield facility this year, part of a larger INR5,000+ crore investment over 7-10 years.

• An additional INR500 crores will be invested in the captive finance division over the next 12-15 months, following an initial INR200 crore investment, with a long-term ROA aspiration of 1.5-2%.

• The Construction Equipment segment anticipates short-term challenges due to macro factors but expects a turnaround in H2 FY27 and strong medium-term demand from public infrastructure development.

Dabur India: Guidance - Concall Update

• Management revised their FY27 revenue growth guidance from high single-digit to low double-digit, anticipating growth from both pricing and volume.

• They expect sequential acceleration of growth in the India business, driven by stable consumption, GTM transformation, premiumization, and brand investments.

• Margins are expected to improve year-on-year, with inflation mitigation through price increases, product mix, savings, and premiumization initiatives.

• The HPC portfolio is expected to achieve double-digit growth in the next year, with oral care specifically targeted for strong double-digit growth in Q1.

• Food and Beverages are targeted for double-digit growth for the full year, contingent on favorable weather conditions.

Blue Star: Guidance - Concall Update

Management is cautiously optimistic for FY27, with the outlook heavily dependent on the summer season's duration and intensity.

For Segment 1 (Electro-Mechanical Projects and Commercial Air Conditioning), the outlook for Commercial AC is 8% to 10% growth, driven by manufacturing, while data center MEP business is expected to more than double within three years, potentially growing from INR1,000 crore to INR3,000 crore annually.

 The company aims to maintain Segment 1 margins at 7% to 7.5% and Segment 2 (Unitary Products) margins at 8% to 8.5% for the current financial year, despite anticipated margin pressure from rising input costs and volatile exchange rates.

Annual capital expenditure is projected to be in the range of INR250 crores to INR350 crores, covering routine CapEx, R&D, product development, and IT/digital investments.

The Room Air Conditioner industry is expected to grow at an 18% to 20% CAGR, potentially reaching 40 million to 50 million units by 2030, with FY27 projected at 17.5 million units, but margins will remain under pressure due to competition and commodity prices.

Radico Khaitan: Guidance - Concall Update

Co achieved net revenue exceeding INR6,000 crores and EBITDA crossing INR1,000 crores in FY'26, with the luxury portfolio delivering INR475 crores in sales value, in line with guidance.

For FY'27, the company expects its luxury portfolio to sustain 25% growth and the Prestige & Above portfolio volume to grow by 20%.

EBITDA margin is projected to expand by 125 basis points for the full year FY'27, driven by price increases (60 bps) and product premiumization (over 200 bps), despite potential commodity cost impacts.

Co aims to become debt-free in FY'27 and has articulated a dividend policy with a minimum payout of 20% of profit after tax.

For the regular segment, a volume growth of 3% to 5% is expected for FY'27, while the non-IMFL business is anticipated to grow at 7% to 8%.

reports.text:
HERBICIDE PARAQUAT DICHLORIDE FACES BAN OVER SEVERE TOXICITY 

EXPERT PANEL LINKS CHEMICAL TO LUNG, KIDNEY ISSUES, PARKINSON'S DISEASE - BL ( DHANUKA AGRITECH , DHARMAJ CROP GUARD )

ONE 97 COMMUNICATIONS MANAGEMENT GUIDANCE UPDATE

FY27 Outlook
- Management expects revenue growth acceleration in FY27  
- Growth to be driven by:
  - Payments business momentum  
  - Financial services expansion  
  - Recovery in marketing services  

Profitability Targets
- Targeting EBITDA margins of 15% to 20% over the next 2.5 to 3 years  
- Operating leverage expected to improve steadily  

Cost Structure
- Indirect costs expected to grow significantly lower than:
  - Revenue growth  
  - Contribution profit growth  

- Focus remains on improving efficiency and scalability  

Customer Acquisition Strategy
- Promotional and cashback spending to continue in a calibrated manner  
- Incentives will remain within existing ratio framework  
- Focus on:
  - High quality customer acquisition  
  - User engagement improvement  

AI Strategy
- Incremental investments planned in AI capabilities  
- AI to enhance customer and merchant experience across existing businesses  
- No major capital expenditure planned for data centers  

Management View
- Strong long-term confidence in payments and financial services ecosystem  
- AI driven operational enhancement expected to support profitability and engagement  

Final Takeaway
- Paytm is focusing on profitable growth through operating leverage, AI integration and scaling financial services while maintaining disciplined customer acquisition spending  

Impact
- Positive

KENNAMETAL INDIA Q4 FY26 UPDATE

Q4 FY26 Performance
- Revenue ₹403.1 Cr vs ₹289.6 Cr (↑ 39% YoY)  
- PBT ₹69.2 Cr vs ₹32.8 Cr (↑ 111% YoY)  
- PAT ₹51.4 Cr vs ₹24.4 Cr (↑ 111% YoY)  

Sequential Performance
- Revenue ₹403.1 Cr vs ₹334.0 Cr (↑ 21% QoQ)  
- PBT ₹69.2 Cr vs ₹35.3 Cr (↑ 96% QoQ)  
- PAT ₹51.4 Cr vs ₹24.4 Cr (↑ 111% QoQ)  

Business Drivers
- Strong industrial manufacturing demand  
- Growth in precision machining and engineering sectors  
- Volume expansion supported by diversified product portfolio  
- Favorable macro environment aiding demand momentum  

Operational Focus
- Active management of tungsten supply chain  
- Tungsten remains a critical raw material for carbide tooling and precision machining products  

Industry Opportunity
- Positioned to benefit from rising manufacturing activity across:
  - EV manufacturing  
  - Aerospace  
  - Defence  
  - Railways  
  - Semiconductors  
  - Heavy engineering  

Strategic Importance
- Carbide tooling and precision machining solutions becoming critical enablers for India’s advanced manufacturing ecosystem  
- Demand expected to rise with increasing industrial automation and localization trends  

Final Takeaway
- Strong earnings growth and rising industrial demand highlight Kennametal India’s positioning in the expanding precision manufacturing and tooling ecosystem  

Impact
- Positive

SHAKTI PUMPS Q4 FY26 UPDATE

Solar Pump Execution
- Installed 86,086 solar pumps in FY26 (↑ 20% YoY)  
- Q4FY26 installations jumped 51% YoY to 28,345 units  
- Strong execution driven by Maharashtra and improving traction from other states  

Revenue Performance
- Solar Pumps business revenue ₹2,080.6 Cr in FY26 (↑ 7% YoY)  
- Q4 Solar Pumps revenue ₹704 Cr (↑ 42% YoY)  

PM KUSUM Update
- MNRE extended PM KUSUM commissioning deadline to March 31, 2027 for orders issued till December 31, 2025  
- Extension linked with proposed PM KUSUM 2.0 rollout  

Receivables Improvement
- Receivables reduced sharply to ₹1,275.7 Cr from ₹1,679 Cr in December 2025  
- Improvement achieved despite record quarterly revenue  
- Company moderated execution pace to improve collection efficiency  

Receivables Quality
- Around 72% receivables are not yet due  
- About 21% receivables overdue by less than 180 days  

Management View
- Focus remains on balancing execution growth with working capital discipline  
- Strong state level demand and policy support continue to support solar pump opportunity  

Final Takeaway
- Strong execution momentum, improving receivable profile and PM KUSUM extension strengthen medium-term growth visibility for the solar pumps business  

Impact
- Positive

FAIRCHEM ORGANICS Q4 FY26 CONCALL HIGHLIGHTS

FY26 Performance
- Revenue ₹460 Cr (↓ 14.5% YoY)  
- EBITDA ₹22 Cr  
- PAT ₹6.2 Cr  
- Volumes declined to 44,000 tons from 54,000 tons YoY  
- Domestic business contributed 91% of revenue  

Q4 FY26
- Revenue ₹117 Cr  
- EBITDA Margin improved to 6.9%  
- Adjusted PAT ₹3.7 Cr  

Business Updates
- Chinese dumping pressure eased from late February  
- Realizations improved 8% to 10%  
- Energy-saving initiatives reduced power and fuel costs  
- Paint industry demand recovery started from March  

Expansion Plans
- New specialty chemical plant expected by Q2 FY27  
- Additional capacity of 40,000 tons  
- New bypass fat plant to commence next month  
- Revenue contribution expected from Q3 FY27  

Growth Outlook
- Isostearic Acid exports expected to scale after tariff resolution  
- Export contribution targeted above 20% over next year  
- Capacity utilization expected at 75% to 80% in FY27  
- EBITDA margin guidance above 8% near term with gradual move into double digits  
- Long-term revenue target ₹2,000 Cr by FY30  

Working Capital
- Working capital cycle expected in 100 to 120 day range  

Final Takeaway
- Recovery in realizations, easing Chinese pressure and upcoming capacity additions expected to improve growth and profitability trajectory  

Impact
- Positive

BSE Q4 FY26 CONCALL HIGHLIGHTS

Financial Highlights
- FY26 total revenue crossed ₹5,148 Cr for the first time (↑ 59% YoY)  
- Net Profit ₹2,497 Cr (↑ 88% YoY)  
- Profit margin expanded to 49% from 41%  
- Q4 FY26 marked 13th consecutive quarter of record revenue at ₹1,630 Cr  
- Dividend recommended ₹10/share  
- Total dividend payout increased 67% YoY to ₹412 Cr  

Business Performance
- Ranked #1 globally in IPO listings during FY26  
- 255 IPOs raised ₹1.8 lakh Cr  
- IPO pipeline remains strong with 250+ active applications  
- Index derivatives average daily premium turnover ↑ 118% to ₹19,523 Cr  
- Sensex options among world’s most actively traded contracts  

Star MF Platform
- Processed record 84 Cr transactions (↑ 27% YoY)  
- Revenue reached ₹285 Cr  
- Monthly peak transaction volume touched 8.2 Cr in March 2026  
- Star NPS platform launched for retirement planning solutions  
- Department of Post onboarded with 5,500+ transactions completed  

Strategic Initiatives
- Launching new monthly index derivatives including IT index contracts from May 11, 2026  
- Exploring commodity derivatives opportunities  
- Increasing technology investments to support peak volumes of 69,000 trades/sec  
- Capex budget may double from earlier ₹300 Cr plan  
- Evaluating land acquisition in Mumbai for future expansion  
- Contribution to Settlement Guarantee Fund reduced to 3.5%  

Final Takeaway
- Strong growth across trading, IPO and mutual fund ecosystems with aggressive technology investments supporting long-term scalability  

Impact
- Positive

Capex Amount for 1GW

Module- 100-150Cr 
Cell+ Module- 500-600Cr
Ingot&Wafer +Cell+ Module- 1100-1200Cr
Poly to Module- 1500-1600Cr 

Capital Intensity increases as you go up from Just Module Manufacturer to Integrated Player.
10x More Capex Heavy than just Module. 

India first incentivesed Module Manufactures, Then Cell and Now moving towards Ingot & Wafer as well. 

Those who will be integrated player, only will be able to catch this train.

ICICI SEC ON BAJAJ AUTO

𝘉𝘶𝘺 

T𝘢𝘳𝘨𝘦𝘵 𝘰𝘧 𝘙𝘴 12,000

Bajaj Auto (BJAUT) delivered a strong Q4FY26, surpassing expectations with revenue growing 32% YoY to ₹160.1bn and EBITDAM at 20.8% (30bps above estimates). 

The company sees domestic motorcycle industry growth moderating to ~7-9% in the near term due to price hikes and weakening consumer sentiment. 

However, export demand for both 2-wheelers (2Ws) and 3-wheelers (3Ws) remains robust, with BJAUT outperforming industry growth in its top export markets. 🏍

Cryogenic OGS forays into precision components manufacturing 🚆⚡

The company has incorporated Infravolt Engineering Pvt Ltd as a subsidiary to expand into manufacturing precision components for power, energy, and railway infrastructure applications.

Key highlights:
✅ Cryogenic OGS to hold 51% stake
✅ Target sectors: Power, Energy & Railways
✅ Focus on backward integration
✅ Paid-up capital: ₹5 lakh
✅ Strategic fit with existing oil & gas solutions business

This move can help Cryogenic OGS deepen its manufacturing ecosystem and widen its addressable industrial opportunity.

Two interesting points from Paytm’s results:

- Paytm says it is not interested in a balance sheet lending play and will continue to remain a loan distributor for other NBFCs and banks. This comes as rival PhonePe has applied for an NBFC licence for the third time.

- It also wants to retain its $1.4 bn cash balance for acquisitions “at the right price and only where strategically additive.” Paytm has not made any sizable acquisitions since its IPO in 2021. AI-based platforms are one area it is exploring.

*~~Citi*

*India Electric Utilities: Switched-On Capex Cycle: Initiate NTPC, PGCIL, TTPW, JSWE at Buy*

India’s first-ever multi-vector capex upcycle – straddling thermal, renewables, transmission, and grid storage – continues to chug along. It remains a multi-year visible story, with the breadth of power demand gradually expanding. Our medium-term forecast is set at a 5-6% CAGR on the strength of electrification, data centres, cooling load, and policy-supported manufacturing sectors. To boot, regulators’ focus has pivoted from capacity to reliability/flexibility, and favorable policies are providing greater visibility. Against this structural change in backdrop, we initiate at Buy on NTPC (TP Rs485), TTPW (Rs525), PGRD (Rs380), JSWE (Rs650) – in that order of preference.

*Stock views: NTPC > Tata Power >PGRD > JSWE — Although power-sector stocks have re-rated in the past month on a power-demand pickup, we believe they still have upside potential. None are materially exposed to merchant price volatility.*

- *NTPC:* Duration/diversity of its regulated business and its multi-modal generation exposure with a cost of funds advantage position it well.

- *Tata Power:* Multi-year transition, with a growing share of lower-volatility businesses as capex stays focused on T&D and renewables.

- *PGRD:* Direct beneficiary of transmission grid buildout in India. Commissioning and capex trajectories have improved.

- *JSWE:* Well placed to benefit from both thermal and renewable buildout.

*Key risks* — a) Execution slippages; b) sustained unfavorable demand variability; c) adverse regulatory developments.

India’s Energy Edge: Optionality

India’s energy security is no longer about self-sufficiency; it’s about strategic optionality. Importing over 85% of its crude, India has navigated the West Asia crisis by diversifying its basket—shifting from Middle East reliance to making Russia its largest supplier (36%). While Brent crude hovers near $109, India’s ability to pivot between Iraq, Saudi Arabia, and the US has provided a vital macroeconomic cushion.

However, the "new oil" order brings fresh risks. As we transition toward EVs and solar, our dependence moves from oil chokepoints like the Strait of Hormuz to critical mineral supply chains dominated by China. Resilience now requires more than just filling strategic reserves; it demands securing the lithium and cobalt needed for the next decade of growth. Diversification is our only shield in a fragmented market.

SEIL Receives 5-Year Renewal of MES Approval

Steel Exchange India Ltd. has received renewal of approval from the Military Engineer Services (MES) under the Ministry of Defence for supply of SIMHADRI TMT bars for the next 5 years.

Update Highlights:
* Approval renewed for Fe 500D and Fe 500D HCRM TMT bars from 8 mm to 32 mm.
* Products are manufactured at SEIL’s integrated Vizianagaram plant using TEMPCORE technology.
* Renewal follows MES quality checks, technical evaluations, and inspection standards.
* MES approvals are granted only to manufacturers meeting stringent quality requirements.
* Approval remains subject to periodic inspections and IS 1786:2008 compliance.
* SEIL continues to remain eligible for MES-linked infrastructure and defence projects.

Strategic Alignment:
The renewal strengthens SEIL’s position in institutional and government-linked segments while reinforcing its focus on quality standards, operational discipline, and participation in high entry-barrier projects.

reports.text:
Thailand's Land Bridge: A Game-Changer for Global Trade?

Thailand is pushing ahead with its ambitious $30B+ Land Bridge — a 90km multimodal corridor linking the Andaman Sea to the Gulf of Thailand. The goal? Create a faster, cheaper overland shortcut that bypasses the congested and vulnerable Strait of Malacca, shaving 4-5 days off shipping times and ~15% off costs.

In an era of Red Sea/Hormuz disruptions and growing worries about maritime chokepoints, this project isn't just infrastructure — it's strategic resilience. If executed well, it could reposition Thailand as a key logistics hub in the Indo-Pacific, challenging Singapore's dominance and reshaping supply chains.

Decades in discussion, now gaining real momentum. The big questions remain: execution, environment, and geopolitics. Worth watching closely.

Join our WhatsApp Channel. 🙏🏻

👉 https://whatsapp.com/channel/0029VbBm3EiKWEKxIsMdoz2X

Politicians: The Demographic of Disconnect

While the common man pours into SIPs, India’s political class remains allergic to the demat account. Out of ₹29,032 crore in disclosed assets across five states, a staggering 99% is locked in land, buildings, and gold. Only a minuscule 0.97% sits in regulated capital markets—a stark contrast to US Congress members who often hold 50-80% of their wealth in stocks.

The data reveals a "sideline" mentality: only 461 out of 7,325 candidates disclosed any market exposure. For a leadership pushing a "Viksit Bharat" built on financialization, their personal portfolios tell a different story. They aren't betting on India’s corporate growth; they are betting on the soil beneath it. The biggest underserved market for financial advisors? The very people making the policies.
